⚠️ Two secrets are verified active — the AWS key has IAM access, and the RSA private key is your JWT signing key. Rotate both within the next 30 minutes.

🔑 Secrets exposed in repo
Critical
AWS Access Key — VERIFIED ACTIVE
Production AWS credentials committed to .env.production. Verified live via STS. IAM user is named deploy-bot and likely has S3 + ECS permissions.
.env.production:14 · committed 47 days ago · AKIA****QF7M (redacted)
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIA****QF7MAWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=**** # ROTATE NOWAWS_REGION=us-east-1
Rotate now →
Critical
JWT signing key — RSA private key in source
Anyone with this key can forge JWTs and impersonate any user, including admins. Rotate the key, invalidate all existing JWTs, and force re-login.
src/auth/jwt.ts:3 · committed 6 months ago · RSA-2048
Rotate + invalidate →
High
Stripe live key in test fixtures
A live Stripe key is in tests/fixtures/seed.ts. If tests run against live Stripe, you're charging real cards in CI. Move to test mode keys (sk_test_*).
tests/fixtures/seed.ts:42 · sk_live_****dPq3
Replace with sk_test_ →

🐛 Code vulnerabilities
Critical
Missing auth check on admin endpoint
/api/admin/users is registered but has no authentication middleware. Anyone with the URL can list, create, or delete users.
src/api/admin.ts:67 · CWE-306 · missing-auth-check
router.post('/api/admin/users', async (req, res) => {  // TODO: add auth  const user = await createUser(req.body);  res.json(user);});
Add auth →
Critical
SQL injection via raw query string interpolation
User input is interpolated directly into SQL. Use parameterized queries or an ORM. This is the #1 OWASP issue and you'll fail any pentest with this in code.
src/api/users.ts:84 · CWE-89 · tainted-sql-string
const { email } = req.query;const result = await db.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = '${email}'`);res.json(result);
Parameterize →
Medium
XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML in email template
User-provided content is rendered as HTML in transactional emails. Sanitize via DOMPurify or escape at render time.
src/templates/email.ts:23 · CWE-79 · dangerously-set-inner-html
Sanitize →
Medium
Permissive CORS configuration
CORS is set to origin: "*" with credentials enabled — browsers will refuse, but it indicates a permissive intent. Restrict to known origins.
src/middleware/cors.ts:11 · CWE-942 · cors-misconfiguration
Restrict origins →

📦 Vulnerable dependencies
Critical
axios 0.21.1 → CSRF (CVE-2023-45857)
Cross-site request forgery in axios via cookie handling. Patched in 1.6.0+.
CVSS 9.1 · npm i axios@^1.7.0
Upgrade →
Critical
jsonwebtoken 8.5.1 → RCE (CVE-2022-23529)
Arbitrary code execution via crafted secretOrPublicKey. Patched in 9.0.0.
CVSS 7.6 · npm i jsonwebtoken@^9.0.2
Upgrade →
High
next 13.4.0 → cache poisoning (CVE-2024-46982)
Crafted x-now-route-matches header allows cache poisoning. Patched in 14.2.13.
CVSS 7.5 · npm i next@^14.2.13
Upgrade →

+ 11 more (lodash, semver, ws, follow-redirects, ...). Run vuln_prioritize for a full sequenced upgrade plan.
